mission.title: openfga: Getting deadline_exceeded response and running out of db conns
mission.description: Getting deadline_exceeded response and running out of db conns. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify openfga troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your openfga deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfga -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga -n openfga --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review openfga configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant openfga configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n openfga -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga
kubectl get configmap -n openfga -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=openfga
```
We've run into a problem where all fga requests fail with deadline_exceeded error response, including even the health check!

We've done some experimentation and we've found the folllowing:

- When this happens the database connections are consumed

steps.title: Apply the fix for Getting deadline_exceeded response and running out of db…
steps.description:
1. Fix cases where Iterators were not being cleaned up (thus not releasing connections or triggering the iterator cache etc) via abstraction (and missing explicit calls).
2. Move SQL that yielded iterators to async. Otherwise, if you need to create 100 iterators, but can only process 5 at the same
```yaml
{"code":"deadline_exceeded","message":"Request Deadline Exceeded"}
```

steps.title: Confirm Getting deadline_exceeded response and running… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=openfga -n openfga --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n openfga --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
1. Fix cases where Iterators were not being cleaned up (thus not releasing connections or triggering the iterator cache etc) via abstraction (and missing explicit calls).
2. Move SQL that yielded iterators to async.
codeSnippets:
openfga:
    depends_on:
      - openfga-migrate
    image: openfga/openfga:v1.8.11
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    command: run
    ports:
      - "48080:8080"
      - "48081:8081"
      - "13000:3000"
    environment:
      - OPENFGA_DATASTORE_ENGINE=postgres
      - OPENFGA_DATASTORE_URI=postgres://openfga:openfga@${DB_ADDRESS}:5432/openfga
      - OPENFGA_LOG_FORMAT=json
      - OPENFGA_PLAYGROUND_PORT=13000
      - OPENFGA_LOG_LEVEL=debug

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with openfga installed or the issue environment reproducible.
